data.narrative:
Boom Supersonic is a Series B stage company in the Supersonic & Hypersonic sector, currently valued at $1.5B post-money following $600M+ in total fundraising. The comparable universe for this segment is sparse, consisting of 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 2 private peers (Hermeus, Venus Aerospace), though same-stage comparables within the narrow sector are insufficient for reliable peer-median analysis.

Given data constraints, stage-progression methodology was the applicable triangulation approach. Applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B progression to the $1.5B last post-money valuation yields $2.9B. This single method anchors the analysis and, with conservative and aggressive bound scenarios, supports a valuation range of $2.4B (low) to $3.3B (high), with $2.9B as the median implied value.

Key uncertainties center on the sparsity of true sector comparables—the Supersonic & Hypersonic category contains only one same-sector peer datapoint at narrow filters, precluding median peer triangulation. Stage-progression multipliers, while grounded in historical Series B dynamics, may not fully capture frontier-tech execution risk or the capital intensity of aerospace development. Near-term funding requirements, regulatory pathway clarity, and manufacturing readiness should inform final valuation placement within the $2.4B–$3.3B range.
